channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: This was an England we haven't seen for years - and it was fun
item.description: Fun to watch and full of intent - this was an England we have not seen for years, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: Tactical analysis: England look exciting but how can they tighten up?
item.description: England produced some exciting attacking football while beating Croatia 4-2 but why did they look so shaky defensively?

item.title: Lionesses will face Greece in World Cup play-offs
item.description: England will face Greece in round one of the 2027 Women's World Cup play-offs, with Northern Ireland, Scotland and Wales also learning their opponents.

item.title: Calvin Harris & Commonwealths force Monday start to Celtic's title defence
item.description: Celtic open the 2026-27 Scottish Premiership season at home to Dundee on 3 August, with the champions saying they are disappointed to be starting on a Monday evening.

item.title: Hydration boos and surprise results - World Cup talking points
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at the talking points from the first week of World Cup.

item.title: Most shots? Best dribbler? World Cup so far in numbers
item.description: Now every team has played once, BBC Sport take a look at who has been the best and worst players so far according to the stats.

item.title: What's going on with stoppage time at the World Cup?
item.description: Pierluigi Collina decided to wage war on time-wasting at this World Cup, changing tack from the last tournament. But has it been a success so far?

item.title: Ivory Coast's Wahi denied entry to Canada
item.description: The Ivory Coast football federation have announced their forward Elye Wahi has been denied entry to Canada for their next World Cup game.

item.title: From one to 48 - every World Cup team ranked after first game
item.description: All 48 teams at the World Cup have now played once. BBC Sport's experts have ranked them all, from best to worst. Who is top? Not defending champions Argentina.

item.title: England hit four in World Cup opener against Croatia
item.description: England make a winning start to their World Cup 2026 campaign, beating Croatia 4-2 in a thrilling match in Dallas.

item.title: Get your predictions in before the second round of World Cup group games
item.description: Play BBC Sport's new World Cup predictor game to be in with the chance of winning a signed football.

item.title: Diaz scores as Colombia beat debutants Uzbekistan
item.description: Colombia make a winning return to the World Cup, defeating debutants Uzbekistan.

item.title: Ghana score late winner to break Panama hearts
item.description: Ghana's Caleb Yirenkyi scored a 95th-minute winner to deny Panama their first-ever World Cup point in Toronto, Canada.

item.title: Messi, Mbappe & Vinicius Jr - best World Cup goals from round one
item.description: Watch the best goals from the first round of games at the World Cup, including memorable strikes from Argentina's Lionel Messi, France's Kylian Mbappe and Brazil's Vinicius Jr.

item.title: Are goalkeepers struggling with the World Cup ball?
item.description: Joe Hart, Wayne Rooney and Mark Chapman discuss whether goalkeepers are struggling to get to grips with the World Cup ball, after a number of long range goals.

item.title: Fayzullaev scores Uzbekistan's first-ever World Cup goal
item.description: Abbosbek Fayzullaev scores Uzbekistan's first-ever goal at a World Cup final, tapping in an equaliser against Colombia.

item.title: Konate to join Real Madrid on four-year deal
item.description: Real Madrid agree a deal to sign Ibrahima Konate on a four-year contract when the France centre-back leaves Liverpool at the end of June.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 11
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: 'Chip on shoulder' may help Bellingham to best form
item.description: Jude Bellingham says the external "noise" around his place in the England team may help him find his best form during the World Cup.

item.title: Nearly man McInnes finally has Rangers job he always wanted
item.description: Why has Derek McInnes swapped nearly winning the Scottish title with Heart of Midlothian for a Rangers side who finished behind them in third last season?

item.title: 'If we lose, we lose in our way' - how Tuchel inspired 'fearful' England
item.description: England players were on the receiving end of a half-time reproach from manager Thomas Tuchel as their World Cup campaign got off to a victorious start against Croatia.

item.title: Christie chases dream on same stage as idol Messi
item.description: Making a World Cup debut was a huge thrill for midfielder Ryan Christie as the Scotland midfielder sets sights on making progress beyond the group stage.

item.title: How to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: Liverpool trigger winger Munoz's £34.5m release clause
item.description: Liverpool are set to sign winger Victor Munoz from Osasuna after triggering his release clause.

item.title: 'The team needs to score, not you' - Ronaldo struggles as rivals sparkle
item.description: Three superstars delivered for their countries at the World Cup on Tuesday. But Cristiano Ronaldo could not follow suit on Wednesday as Portugal were held to a surprise draw by DR Congo.

item.title: Working with Postecoglou and following the data - Salford's new boss Cklamovski
item.description: Salford City have appointed Peter Cklamovski, someone with no experience of coaching in English football to try and lead them to promotion out of League Two

item.title: Who was the biggest attacking threat? England player ratings
item.description: Who was England's biggest attacking threat against Croatia? Alex Howell rates the players - plus have your say.

item.title: Hart impressed by Kane's attention to detail with penalties
item.description: BBC World Cup pundits Joe Hart and Wayne Rooney analyse Harry Kane's change in techniques and the amount of work he puts in when it comes to taking penalties.

item.title: Substitute Rashford guides home England's fourth against Croatia
item.description: Substitute Marcus Rashford scores for England to seal a 4-2 win over Croatia in their opening Group L match at the 2026 Fifa World Cup.

item.title: Bellingham scores early in second half to put England 3-2 up
item.description: Jude Bellingham gives England a flying start to the second half against Croatia, scoring in the 47th minute to put Thomas Tuchel's side 3-2 ahead at the Fifa World Cup.

item.title: Kane heads England into 2-1 lead against Croatia
item.description: Harry Kane scores his second goal of the game against Croatia, heading home from a Declan Rice corner to give England a 2-1 lead.

item.title: Kane puts England ahead against Croatia with retaken penalty
item.description: Harry Kane puts England 1-0 up against Croatia with a retaken penalty, after his initial attempt was saved by Dominik Livakovic, who was deemed to be off his line.

item.title: Musa strikes for Croatia to make it 2-2 against England
item.description: Petar Musa scores for Croatia to make it 2-2 against England in their World Cup match in Dallas.

item.title: Baturina fires Croatia level against England
item.description: Croatia's Martin Baturina scores from outside the box to make it 1-1 against England in their group L game at the Dallas Stadium in the Fifa World Cup.

item.title: DR Congo earn World Cup point against Portugal
item.description: DR Congo come from behind to hold Portugal to a 1-1 draw in their Group K match at the Houston Stadium in the Fifa World Cup.

item.title: I knew stuttered run-up might get keeper off line - Kane
item.description: Harry Kane was able to give England the lead against Croatia from the penalty spot - but only thanks to the video assistant referee.

item.title: Man Utd & Spurs move for Summerville - Thursday's gossip
item.description: Manchester United and Tottenham move for Crysencio Summerville, Chelsea monitor goalkeeper Diogo Costa and target Andrea Cambiaso.

item.title: Drone brought down near South Korea training base
item.description: The Mexican military reportedly intercepts and brings down an unregistered drone seen flying over South Korea's training base before their Group A match with co-hosts Mexico on Friday.

item.title: How sweltering Scotland can handle World Cup heat
item.description: With Scotland's World Cup games expected to take place in temperatures of around 30C, Steve Clarke and his team will have to battle the conditions as well as Morocco and Brazil.

item.title: Real Madrid sign Silva on free after Man City exit
item.description: Bernado Silva, who left Manchester City at the end of the season, links up with new Real Madrid manager Jose Mourinho.

item.title: Spurs join clubs interested in Tonali
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur have joined the list of clubs who are interested in Newcastle United midfielder Sandro Tonali.

item.title: What are the patches on some shirts at the World Cup?
item.description: England captain Harry Kane was seen wearing a special patch on his England shirt - why are they there and who else gets to wear them?

item.title: Scottish Premiership fixture release - all you need to know
item.description: The fixtures for the 2026-27 Scottish Premiership season are released on Thursday at 12:00 BST - and you can see them on the BBC Sport website and app as soon as they are announced.

item.title: Cape Verde goalkeeper set to be reunited with his mum
item.description: Vozinha's mum has been granted a visa to travel to the US ahead of Cape Verde's next game against Uruguay.

item.title: Extraordinary Messi makes more history in masterclass for the ages
item.description: Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland both enjoyed a fine start to the World Cup - but on a day packed with superstars, Lionel Messi still took top billing.

item.title: Fulham 'really like' two candidates to replace Silva
item.description: Fulham have two leading candidates in mind to take over from former manager Marco Silva, says the club's vice-chairman and director of football operations Tony Khan.

item.title: Tottenham sign Scotland forward Hanson from Villa
item.description: Tottenham have signed Scotland international Kirsty Hanson from Aston Villa for an undisclosed fee.

item.title: Hearts, Hibs and Motherwell learn European opponents
item.description: Heart of Midlothian will face Sturm Graz in the second round of Champions League qualifying as Hibernian and Motherwell learn their Conference League opponents.

item.title: Fans boo, players adapt - the view on World Cup hydration breaks
item.description: Supporters are booing them, coaches are using them, players are adapting to them - mid-half hydration breaks are affecting everyone at the World Cup in different ways.

item.title: Project Mbappe - the road to becoming France's record scorer
item.description: Kylian Mbappe has become France's all-time record scorer, aged just 27. BBC Sport takes a closer look at how he got here.

item.title: No stage too big - Haaland arrives in style at World Cup
item.description: Erling Haaland has made a habit of scoring debut goals in the big competitions. He did it in the Champions League, the Bundesliga and the Premier League. Now he has done it at the World Cup too.

item.title: False nines? 4-4-2? The tactical trends defining World Cup so far
item.description: The return of 4-4-2? False nines? BBC Sport looks at the tactical trends emerging at the World Cup so far.

item.title: World Cup hydration breaks - who are the winners and losers?
item.description: They have become a regular feature at World Cup games - but who are the winners and who are losers of the mandatory hydration breaks?

item.title: The 40-year-old keeper who inspired Cape Verde's historic debut against Spain
item.description: Cape Verde stuns Spain with a goalless draw inspired by a 40-year-old goalkeeper who has gone viral on social media.

item.title: Who will win the World Cup? BBC pundits make their predictions
item.description: Who will win, who might spring a surprise and how will England and Scotland do? BBC pundits predict what will happen at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Will three points guarantee a spot in World Cup knockouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at whether earning three points in the World Cup group stage will be enough to advance to the knockout rounds.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: How to watch highlights of World Cup matches
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how you can watch match highlights and full match replays during the 2026 Fifa World Cup

item.title: Why are footballers wearing pink boots at the World Cup?
item.description: The opening match of the 2026 World Cup was dominated by players wearing pink boots - but why is the colour so prevalent in football at the moment?

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Scottish Premiership fixtures - find out your club's schedule
item.description: Find out your team's schedule for this season's Scottish Premiership pre-split campaign.

item.title: Why does Scotland's McGinn do goggles celebration?
item.description: John McGinn opens up about the relative who inspired his goal celebration.

item.title: What are Clarke's big calls for Morocco? And who would you pick?
item.description: Read what the pundits think and pick your own XI as Scotland prepare to face Morocco.

item.title: Scotland draw Czech Republic in round one of World Cup play-offs
item.description: Scotland will have to beat Czech Republic and either Lithuania or Sweden in successive two-legged play-off ties if they are to reach next summer's Women's World Cup.

item.title: Weir signs for Champions League runners-up Lyon
item.description: Scotland captain Caroline Weir signs a three-year contract with French champions Lyon after leaving Real Madrid.

item.title: Six Lionesses made MBEs in King's Birthday Honours
item.description: Kevin Sinfield has received a knighthood in the King's Birthday Honours while six of England's Euro 2025-winning squad have been made MBEs.

item.title: Free agent Wales players put themselves in shop window
item.description: Rhian Wilkinson says her free-agent internationals gave potential suitors a glimpse of their qualities by helping Wales finish top of their Women's World Cup qualifying group.

item.title: Man City sign England's Mead on free transfer
item.description: Manchester City have confirmed the signing of England forward Beth Mead on a free transfer from Arsenal.

item.title: Weir's title regret as she confirms Real Madrid exit
item.description: Scotland midfielder Caroline Weir has confirmed her exit from Real Madrid after four years with the La Liga runners-up without giving a hint of her next destination.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
